title: Fagara tragodes
intro: Fagara tragodes, also known as Chinese prickly ash or Zanthoxylum bungeanum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and other parts of Asia. The health benefits of Fagara tragodes include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara tragodes is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

side_effects: Fagara tragodes contains compounds that can cause gastrointestinal irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingestion of the plant may lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain. Fagara tragodes may also cause skin irritation and allergic contact dermatitis in people who come into contact with its sap or other plant parts. The plant's volatile oils can cause respiratory issues, including coughing and shortness of breath, in individuals who inhale them. Fagara tragodes should be handled with caution and avoided altogether if you have a known allergy or sensitivity to its compounds.
